revision.comment: Text replacement - "|須恵器}}" to "|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}"
revision.text:
{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shirotori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shirotori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shirotori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of {{ill|Shidami Kofungun|ja|志段味古墳群}}| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shirotori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shirotori Kofungun) is a collection of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|ja|志段味古墳群}}.
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along {{ill|National Route 155 (Japan)|ja|国道155号}}, this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shirotori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shirotori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳}} * {{ill|Shirotori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳}}

{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shirotori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shirotori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shirotori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of {{ill|Shidami Kofungun|ja|志段味古墳群}}| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shirotori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shirotori Kofungun) is a collection of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|ja|志段味古墳群}}.
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along {{ill|National Route 155 (Japan)|ja|国道155号}}, this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shirotori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shirotori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳}} * {{ill|Shirotori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳}}

The '''Shirotori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shirotori Kofungun) is a collection of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|ja|志段味古墳群}}.

{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}

Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shirotori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)

=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop

revision.text:
{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shirotori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shirotori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shirotori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of {{ill|Shidami Kofungun|ja|志段味古墳群}}| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shirotori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shirotori Kofungun) is a collection of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|ja|志段味古墳群}}.
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along {{ill|National Route 155 (Japan)|ja|国道155号}}, this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shirotori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shirotori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳}} * {{ill|Shirotori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|12=draft}} (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|12=draft}} (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|12=draft}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shirotori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shirotori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{otheruses|名古屋市守山区にある[[志段味古墳群]]の一部の古墳|志段味古墳群中の別の古墳|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|名古屋市[[熱田区]]にある古墳|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} {{ 日本の古墳| 画像= [[画像:Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| 画像キャプション= 東谷山白鳥古墳（1号墳）
（2021年（令和3年）1月）| 名称= 白鳥古墳群| 所在地=愛知県名古屋市守山区上志段味字白鳥| 位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E|}}| 形状= 円墳| 規模= 墳径17.5x16.5m、高さ3.5m（東谷山白鳥古墳（1号墳））| 築造年代= 6世紀後半| 史跡指定= 国の[[史跡]]「白鳥古墳群」
（「[[志段味古墳群]]」に包含）| 出土品= 須恵器、土師器、馬具、直刀、刀子、鉄鏃など| }} '''白鳥古墳群'''（しろとりこふんぐん）は[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]][[上志段味]]字白鳥にある複数の[[古墳]]の総称で、[[志段味古墳群]]のひとつに数えられる。 == 概要 == かつては5号墳までの5基とされていたが、近年の調査で8基目に数えられる古墳が確認されている。また、1号墳は市内にある古墳のうち、原形をほぼ完全に残した石室を持つ事で知られる。 {{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = 整備前の白鳥1号墳 | caption1 = 整備前の白鳥1号墳
（2008年（平成20年）3月） | image2 = Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = 白鳥1号墳石室内 | caption2 = 白鳥1号墳石室内
（2021年（令和3年）1月） }} === 東谷山白鳥古墳（1号墳） === [[国道155号]]沿いに立地しながら破壊を免れた墳径17.5メートル（東西）16.5メートル（南北）、高さ3.5メートルの[[円墳]]で、全長9.8メートル、玄室長4.2メートル、最大幅1.6メートル、高さ2.4メートルの平面徳利型・無袖式の[[横穴式石室]]を持つ。[[羨道]]の長さは3.2メートルで西側に開口している。6世紀後半に築造されたと考えられており、名古屋市内の古墳としては[[石室]]をほぼ完全に残している稀有な例である。[[平成]]7年（[[1995年]]）に名古屋市の史跡に指定された。その後、2014年（平成26年）10月6日に「東谷山白鳥古墳」の名称で国の史跡「志段味古墳群」に追加指定され[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf 「史跡志段味古墳群保存管理計画」]、名古屋市教育委員会、2015 P.139、[[2021年]]（[[令和]]3年）10月11日に追加指定とともに「白鳥古墳群」に名称変更がなされた令和3年10月11日文部科学省告示第164号。。 大正時代に羨道部の土砂を搬出して調査が行われた際には[[土器]]や[[曲玉]]、[[鉄鏃]]が出土したとの記録がある。また旧守山市による[[昭和]]36年（[[1961年]]）の調査で土器や武具が出土しており、出土品は[[名古屋市博物館]]に収蔵されている。平成18年から19年（[[2006年]]〜[[2007年]]）に[[名古屋市教育委員会]]が墳裾や周囲の[[トレンチ]]掘削や測量など範囲確認調査を行なった際に残存する周溝が検出された。形状はアルファベットのC型で開口部では途切れており、幅約4.9メートル、深さ0.95メートル（背面側の方が若干広い）。これにより周溝を含めた1号墳の規模は東西約25メートル、南北約24.5メートルとなった。また、同調査の際に隣接する位置に8号墳（後述）の遺構を確認した。 なお、現在は石室前に格子戸が設けられ施錠されているため、通常は内部に立ち入ることは出来ない。 ==== 出土品（1961年） ==== * [[須恵器]] - 脚付短頚坩、つまみ付き蓋 2点、平瓶、無蓋高坏、高坏片 * [[土師器]]片 - 少々 * [[馬具]]一式 - [[ハミ (馬具)|轡]]鏡板、手引、手引壷、止金具、銜 * [[直刀]]片 - 3本 * [[刀子]]片 - 2本 * [[鉄鏃]] - 羨道部 1本、玄室部 2本 === 2号墳 === 墳径12メートルの円墳で西向きに開口した横穴式石室を持っていたが、昭和36年の調査後に開発によって滅失した。平瓶が出土している。地表下に周溝が残存している可能性があるとされている。 === 3号墳 === 2号墳と同規模の円墳。開発によって2号墳と同時期に滅失。2号墳同様、地表下に周溝が残存している可能性があるとされている。 === 4号墳 === かつては墳径10メートル、高さ2メートルの円墳とされていたが、平成17年（[[2005年]]）の住宅建築に伴う発掘調査で墳径15メートル、高さ2.1メートルの墳丘残存が確認され、西南西に開口した無袖式の横穴式石室の遺構が検出された。 石室は奥壁から羨道先端部まで6.65メートル、玄室長3.56メートル、石室最大幅1.74メートルなどで、天井石が失われていた。石室内部などから矢穴のある石材片が見つかっている事などから、後年になって石室材が採取され、端材を石室の空洞部に遺棄したと見られている。築造時の推定規模は17.0メートル×16.4メートル。調査後に滅失した。 ==== 出土品（2005年） ==== * 土師器 - [[甕]] 1点 * 須恵器 - 提瓶 2点、無蓋高坏片 * 刀子 - 1本 * 鉄鏃 - 2本以上 * [[灰釉陶器]]椀 - 1点 ※ 以上は玄室内部からの出土。玄室外からは縄文時代の遺物なども見つかった。 === 5号墳 === 墳径10メートル、高さ2.5メートルの円墳。天井石と思われる石材が露出しており、横穴式石室が残存していると思われるが未調査。 === 6号墳 === 民家の庭先にある塚状部で、古墳と推定されているが未調査のため実際のところは不明である。 === 7号墳 === 墳径約10メートルの円墳。4号墳調査時の伐採で墳丘と思しき地形が見つかり、表土中から須恵器の坏蓋が完全な形で見つかった事などから、合わせて調査が行われた。墳頂と南側は削平され北側は国道155号に面した崖状になっているなど形状は大きく損なわれていたが、南側に開口していた石室の西側壁と見られる石積み（残存長3.3メートル、高さ0.4メートル）と須恵器が出土した。調査後、滅失した。 ==== 出土品（2005年） ==== * 須恵器 - 坏蓋 5点、坏身 4点 === 8号墳 === 円墳。平成18年から19年の1号墳調査で、隣接する位置に8号墳の周濠を検出。墳丘の盛土と周濠が地表下に残存する事を確認した。6世紀後半に築造された可能性があるとされる。 ==== 出土品 ==== * 須恵器 - 提瓶 1点 == アクセス == * [[大曽根駅]]（[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央線 (名古屋地区)|中央本線]]、[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄瀬戸線|瀬戸線]]、[[名古屋市営地下鉄]][[名古屋市営地下鉄名城線|名城線]]） * [[高蔵寺駅]]（JR中央本線、[[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]]） ** [[名古屋ガイドウェイバス]]・[[名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|ガイドウェイバス志段味線]]（[[ゆとりーとライン]]）「東谷橋」バス停下車。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『守山の古墳』- 守山市教育委員会、昭和38年（[[1963年]]） * 『名古屋の考古遺跡 守山区』- 三渡俊一郎 著、平成5年（[[1993年]]） * 『名古屋市文化財調査報告68・埋蔵文化財調査報告書53』- 名古屋市見晴台考古史料館・編、名古屋市教育委員会・発行、平成18年（[[2006年]]） * 『[[新修名古屋市史]] 資料編 考古1』- 新修名古屋市史史料編編集委員会・編、名古屋市・発行、平成20年（[[2008年]]） * 『埋蔵文化財調査報告書58 白鳥1号墳・8号墳、正木町遺跡（第21次）、玉ノ井遺跡（第5次）』- 名古屋市見晴台考古史料館・編、名古屋市教育委員会・発行、平成20年（2008年） == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥塚古墳]] * [[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しろとりこふんくん}} [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:志段味古墳群]] [[Category:愛知県にある国指定の史跡]]

（2021年（令和3年）1月） }} === 東谷山白鳥古墳（1号墳） === [[国道155号]]沿いに立地しながら破壊を免れた墳径17.5メートル（東西）16.5メートル（南北）、高さ3.5メートルの[[円墳]]で、全長9.8メートル、玄室長4.2メートル、最大幅1.6メートル、高さ2.4メートルの平面徳利型・無袖式の[[横穴式石室]]を持つ。[[羨道]]の長さは3.2メートルで西側に開口している。6世紀後半に築造されたと考えられており、名古屋市内の古墳としては[[石室]]をほぼ完全に残している稀有な例である。[[平成]]7年（[[1995年]]）に名古屋市の史跡に指定された。その後、2014年（平成26年）10月6日に「東谷山白鳥古墳」の名称で国の史跡「志段味古墳群」に追加指定され[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf 「史跡志段味古墳群保存管理計画」]、名古屋市教育委員会、2015 P.139、[[2021年]]（[[令和]]3年）10月11日に追加指定とともに「白鳥古墳群」に名称変更がなされた令和3年10月11日文部科学省告示第164号。。 大正時代に羨道部の土砂を搬出して調査が行われた際には[[土器]]や[[曲玉]]、[[鉄鏃]]が出土したとの記録がある。また旧守山市による[[昭和]]36年（[[1961年]]）の調査で土器や武具が出土しており、出土品は[[名古屋市博物館]]に収蔵されている。平成18年から19年（[[2006年]]〜[[2007年]]）に[[名古屋市教育委員会]]が墳裾や周囲の[[トレンチ]]掘削や測量など範囲確認調査を行なった際に残存する周溝が検出された。形状はアルファベットのC型で開口部では途切れており、幅約4.9メートル、深さ0.95メートル（背面側の方が若干広い）。これにより周溝を含めた1号墳の規模は東西約25メートル、南北約24.5メートルとなった。また、同調査の際に隣接する位置に8号墳（後述）の遺構を確認した。 なお、現在は石室前に格子戸が設けられ施錠されているため、通常は内部に立ち入ることは出来ない。 ==== 出土品（1961年） ==== * [[須恵器]] - 脚付短頚坩、つまみ付き蓋 2点、平瓶、無蓋高坏、高坏片 * [[土師器]]片 - 少々 * [[馬具]]一式 - [[ハミ (馬具)|轡]]鏡板、手引、手引壷、止金具、銜 * [[直刀]]片 - 3本 * [[刀子]]片 - 2本 * [[鉄鏃]] - 羨道部 1本、玄室部 2本 === 2号墳 === 墳径12メートルの円墳で西向きに開口した横穴式石室を持っていたが、昭和36年の調査後に開発によって滅失した。平瓶が出土している。地表下に周溝が残存している可能性があるとされている。 === 3号墳 === 2号墳と同規模の円墳。開発によって2号墳と同時期に滅失。2号墳同様、地表下に周溝が残存している可能性があるとされている。 === 4号墳 === かつては墳径10メートル、高さ2メートルの円墳とされていたが、平成17年（[[2005年]]）の住宅建築に伴う発掘調査で墳径15メートル、高さ2.1メートルの墳丘残存が確認され、西南西に開口した無袖式の横穴式石室の遺構が検出された。 石室は奥壁から羨道先端部まで6.65メートル、玄室長3.56メートル、石室最大幅1.74メートルなどで、天井石が失われていた。石室内部などから矢穴のある石材片が見つかっている事などから、後年になって石室材が採取され、端材を石室の空洞部に遺棄したと見られている。築造時の推定規模は17.0メートル×16.4メートル。調査後に滅失した。 ==== 出土品（2005年） ==== * 土師器 - [[甕]] 1点 * 須恵器 - 提瓶 2点、無蓋高坏片 * 刀子 - 1本 * 鉄鏃 - 2本以上 * [[灰釉陶器]]椀 - 1点 ※ 以上は玄室内部からの出土。玄室外からは縄文時代の遺物なども見つかった。 === 5号墳 === 墳径10メートル、高さ2.5メートルの円墳。天井石と思われる石材が露出しており、横穴式石室が残存していると思われるが未調査。 === 6号墳 === 民家の庭先にある塚状部で、古墳と推定されているが未調査のため実際のところは不明である。 === 7号墳 === 墳径約10メートルの円墳。4号墳調査時の伐採で墳丘と思しき地形が見つかり、表土中から須恵器の坏蓋が完全な形で見つかった事などから、合わせて調査が行われた。墳頂と南側は削平され北側は国道155号に面した崖状になっているなど形状は大きく損なわれていたが、南側に開口していた石室の西側壁と見られる石積み（残存長3.3メートル、高さ0.4メートル）と須恵器が出土した。調査後、滅失した。 ==== 出土品（2005年） ==== * 須恵器 - 坏蓋 5点、坏身 4点 === 8号墳 === 円墳。平成18年から19年の1号墳調査で、隣接する位置に8号墳の周濠を検出。墳丘の盛土と周濠が地表下に残存する事を確認した。6世紀後半に築造された可能性があるとされる。 ==== 出土品 ==== * 須恵器 - 提瓶 1点 == アクセス == * [[大曽根駅]]（[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央線 (名古屋地区)|中央本線]]、[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄瀬戸線|瀬戸線]]、[[名古屋市営地下鉄]][[名古屋市営地下鉄名城線|名城線]]） * [[高蔵寺駅]]（JR中央本線、[[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]]） ** [[名古屋ガイドウェイバス]]・[[名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|ガイドウェイバス志段味線]]（[[ゆとりーとライン]]）「東谷橋」バス停下車。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『守山の古墳』- 守山市教育委員会、昭和38年（[[1963年]]） * 『名古屋の考古遺跡 守山区』- 三渡俊一郎 著、平成5年（[[1993年]]） * 『名古屋市文化財調査報告68・埋蔵文化財調査報告書53』- 名古屋市見晴台考古史料館・編、名古屋市教育委員会・発行、平成18年（[[2006年]]） * 『[[新修名古屋市史]] 資料編 考古1』- 新修名古屋市史史料編編集委員会・編、名古屋市・発行、平成20年（[[2008年]]） * 『埋蔵文化財調査報告書58 白鳥1号墳・8号墳、正木町遺跡（第21次）、玉ノ井遺跡（第5次）』- 名古屋市見晴台考古史料館・編、名古屋市教育委員会・発行、平成20年（2008年） == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥塚古墳]] * [[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しろとりこふんくん}} [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:志段味古墳群]] [[Category:愛知県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shirotori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shirotori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shirotori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of {{ill|Shidami Kofungun|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shirotori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shirotori Kofungun) is a collection of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along {{ill|National Route 155 (Japan)|ja|国道155号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shirotori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shirotori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shirotori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳群|version=95248077|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shirotori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shirotori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shirotori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of {{ill|Shidami Kofungun|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shirotori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shirotori Kofungun) is a collection of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along {{ill|National Route 155 (Japan)|ja|国道155号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shirotori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shirotori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shirotori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shirotori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shirotori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shirotori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of {{ill|Shidami Kofungun|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofungun|qq=}}| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shirotori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shirotori Kofungun) is a collection of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun group|qq=}}.
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shirotori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shirotori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shirotori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along {{ill|National Route 155 (Japan)|ja|国道155号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 155 (Japan)|qq=}}, this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shirotori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzone Station|qq=}} (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzōji Station|qq=}} (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shirotori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shirotori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori Kofun|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳群|version=95248077|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of {{ill|Shidami Kofungun|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofungun|qq=}}| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun group|qq=}}.
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along {{ill|National Route 155 (Japan)|ja|国道155号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 155 (Japan)|qq=}}, this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzone Station|qq=}} (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzōji Station|qq=}} (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳群|version=95248077|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳群|version=95248077|comment=Imported full ja history}}

=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}

revision.text:
{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳群|version=95248077|comment=Imported full ja history}}

=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)

=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
