The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop

== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Moriyama Ward
revision.text:
{{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=|1=Nagoya Guideway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414812}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}

{{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11580544]] Wikidata name: Shiratori Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=|1=Nagoya Guideway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414812}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop

<!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--> [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Aichi prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shirotori Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11580544}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirotori Kofun Group]][[ja:白鳥古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11580544]] Wikidata name: Shiratori Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|qq=|1=Nagoya Guideway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414812}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}

<!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--><!--Shiratori Kofun Group--> [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Aichi prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shirotori Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11580544}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirotori Kofun Group]][[ja:白鳥古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580544]] Wikidata name: Shiratori Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|1=Nagoya Guideway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414812}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}

It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|1=Nagoya Guideway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414812}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop

== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}

=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|1=Nagoya Guideway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414812}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirotori Kofun Group]][[ja:白鳥古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580544]] Wikidata name: Shiratori Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|1=Nagoya Guideway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414812}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirotori Kofun Group]][[ja:白鳥古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580544]] Wikidata name: Shiratori Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|1=Nagoya Guideway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414812}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirotori Kofun Group]][[ja:白鳥古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580544]] Wikidata name: Shiratori Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|1=Nagoya Guideway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414812}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirotori Kofun Group]][[ja:白鳥古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580544]] Wikidata name: Shiratori Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shidami Kofungun tumulus cluster in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Another tumulus in Shidami Kofungun|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|A tumulus in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun (Atsuta)}} {{ Japanese tumuli| Image=[[File:Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (1), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]| Caption= Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) (2021)| Name= Shiratori Kofun Cluster| Location= Kamishidami, Shirotori, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan| Coordinates= {{Coord|35|15|29.66|N|137|02|43.08|E}}| Type= Circular tumuli| Size= Diameter: 17.5 x 16.5 m, Height: 3.5 m (Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1))| Construction= Late 6th century| Designation= National Historic Site as part of [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]| Artifacts= Sue pottery, Haji pottery, horse fittings, swords, small knives, iron arrowheads| }}
The '''Shiratori Kofun Cluster''' (白鳥古墳群, Shiratori Kofungun) is a collection of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja|守山区上志段味字白鳥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Aichi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun group]].
== Overview == Originally thought to consist of five tumuli, recent surveys have confirmed the existence of an eighth. The No. 1 tumulus is notable for preserving its original stone chamber, a rarity among Nagoya's tumuli.
{{multiple image | footer = | width = 200 | image1 = Shiratori Kofungun01-01.jpg | alt1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 before restoration | caption1 = Shiratori Kofun No. 1 prior to restoration (2008)| | image2 = Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (3), Shirotori Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg | alt2 = Inside the stone chamber of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 | caption2 = Interior of Shiratori Kofun No. 1 (2021) }}
=== Togokusan Shiratori Kofun (No. 1) === Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 155|National Route 155 (Japan)]], this circular tumulus measures 17.5 meters (east-west) by 16.5 meters (north-south) in diameter and has a height of 3.5 meters. It features a horizontal stone chamber of a plain bottle-shaped type without side shoulders, with a total length of 9.8 meters. The chamber’s burial chamber measures 4.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.4 meters high, while the dromos (stone-paved corridor) measures 3.2 meters and opens to the west. Constructed in the late 6th century, it is a rare example of a tumulus in Nagoya with an intact stone chamber.
Designated as a Nagoya City Historic Site in 1995, it was incorporated into the national historic site designation of the Shidami Kofungun in 2014 and renamed the "Shiratori Kofun Cluster" in 2021.<ref>[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf "Shidami Kofungun Conservation and Management Plan"], Nagoya City Board of Education, 2015, p. 139.</ref><ref>Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 164, October 11, 2021.</ref>
=== Artifacts (1961 Survey) === * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] – Various vessels including stemmed jars and plates * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] fragments * Horse fittings – Bits, buckles, and other components * Swords (3 fragments) * Small knives (2 fragments) * Iron arrowheads – 3 (1 in the dromos, 2 in the burial chamber)
=== 2nd Kofun === This circular tumulus, with a diameter of 12 meters, featured a west-facing horizontal stone chamber. Destroyed during post-1961 development, it yielded a Sue pottery flat jar. Potential remnants of its moat may remain underground.
=== 3rd Kofun === Similar in size and fate to the 2nd Kofun, this tumulus was also lost to development after 1961. It too may retain subsurface remnants of its moat.
=== 4th Kofun === Initially thought to have a diameter of 10 meters and a height of 2 meters, excavation in 2005 revealed a preserved mound measuring 15 meters in diameter and 2.1 meters in height. It had a west-southwest-facing horizontal stone chamber with a unique layout, suggesting later modifications and possible reuse.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Haji pottery – One jar * Sue pottery – Two pitchers and other fragments * Small knife – One * Iron arrowheads – Two or more * Gray-glazed pottery bowl – One
=== 5th Kofun === With a diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters, this tumulus is presumed to contain an unexcavated horizontal stone chamber.
=== 6th Kofun === Located in a residential garden, this mound is suspected to be a tumulus but remains uninvestigated.
=== 7th Kofun === Excavated during the 2005 survey, this 10-meter diameter circular tumulus had been partially destroyed. Remnants of a south-facing stone chamber and Sue pottery artifacts were recovered.
=== Artifacts (2005 Survey) === * Sue pottery – Several intact and fragmentary pieces
=== 8th Kofun === Discovered during surveys of the 1st Kofun, this circular tumulus contains remnants of its moat and mound, dating to the late 6th century.
==== Artifacts ==== * Sue pottery – One pitcher
== Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ōzone Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya City Subway Meijō Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, Aichi Loop Line) * {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus|ja|名古屋ガイドウェイバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus|1=Nagoya Guideway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414812}} "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "Moriya no Kofun" – Moriya City Board of Education, 1963 * "Nagoya no Kōko Iseki Moriyama-ku" – Shunichirō Mitobe, 1993 * "Nagoya City Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 68" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2006 * "New Nagoya City History, Archaeology Vol. 1" – Editorial Committee, 2008 * "Shiratori Kofun 1 and 8, Masaki Site (21st Survey), Tamanoi Site (5th Survey)" – Nagoya City Board of Education, 2008 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}}
